title: Race-blocked monotone calibration and blend selection
summary: Post-hoc monotone calibration can preserve ranking while fixing score shape, and Venn-Abers/Beta calibration are stronger than plain Platt in many binary settings.
rationale: Post-hoc monotone calibration can preserve ranking while fixing score shape, and Venn-Abers/Beta calibration are stronger than plain Platt in many binary settings. Because this task is scored on probabilities but selected by AUC, a calibration layer should be fit only on out-of-fold race-blocked predictions to avoid optimistic CV and to stabilize ensembles across regimes.
implementation_hint: Generate strict OOF predictions from each base/specialist model under grouped race or `Race_Year` folds, then compare Beta, isotonic/ROC-regularized isotonic, and Venn-Abers calibrators globally and by broad regime (`dry` vs `wet`, early vs late race). Use the calibrated OOF stack for final blending, not raw in-fold scores.
expected_effect: Small but real gain from desaturating extreme scores and choosing blends on cleaner OOF distributions rather than noisy raw margins.
risk: If calibration folds are too small or not race-blocked, the layer can add variance or fake CV lift.
